title: Exotic Tender Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A malty, tender, one-pot Egyptian dish featuring herbes-de-provence and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients, with a crispy agemono-deep-fry touch. This recipe combines the flavors of Egypt with the principles of ayurvedic cooking.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the herbes-de-provence and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, dates, and prunes to the pot.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
While the tagine is cooking, heat about 1/2 inch of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat for the agemono-deep-fry touch.
When the oil is hot, add a few pieces of the cooked lamb to the skillet and fry until crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
